composition.Programmer 1:
Implement the backend code for the 'GymMembership' web application.
- The website should be accessible via the local port 5000, and the first page can be directly accessed through the route '/'.
- Implement routing for all frontend pages, ensuring each route corresponds to the frontend code.
- File read and write operations must start from the first line of the file, using plain text without encryption.
- Frontend code files to be developed based on this backend 
  - dashboard.html
  - membership_plans.html
  - plan_details.html
  - class_schedule.html
  - trainer_profiles.html
  - trainer_detail.html
  - pt_booking.html
  - workout_records.html
  - log_workout.html
- The website should start from the Dashboard page.
- Data is managed through local text files in the 'data' directory as specified.
composition.Programmer 2:
Develop the frontend code for the 'GymMembership' web application.
- Develop all frontend pages as HTML files with Python route URLs (not static HTML file names).
- Implement the following pages with the specified elements and IDs 
  - Dashboard Page (dashboard.html)
  - Membership Plans Page (membership_plans.html)
  - Plan Details Page (plan_details.html)
  - Class Schedule Page (class_schedule.html)
  - Trainer Profiles Page (trainer_profiles.html)
  - Trainer Detail Page (trainer_detail.html)
  - PT Booking Page (pt_booking.html)
  - Workout Records Page (workout_records.html)
  - Log Workout Page (log_workout.html)
- Ensure navigation buttons use route URLs.
- Follow the detailed page design and element requirements from the original task.
